reasoning: Hikaru Nakamura's official FIDE Elo as of late 2025 is ~2816. A 1% increase equates to an ~28 point gain, which would demand a near-unprecedented winning streak at the elite level to raise his rating to around ~2844 (a level reached only by Carlsen, Kasparov, and Caruana). Considering FIDE rankings generally update on the 1st of each month, the rating on Wikipedia on November 16 will be identical to the rating on November 9, as both fall within the same month's rating list, yielding a 0% change. Even by December 2025 or February 2026, gaining 28 points would require practically impossible overperformance (+7 or +8 wins) in super-tournaments, especially because FIDE eliminated the 'farming' loophole (400-point rule) starting October 2025. Given his age and historical peak (approx. 2816), a 1% surge in his official Elo in a 1-to-3 month timeframe is virtually impossible. I am keeping probabilities near the absolute minimum allowed (0.02), increasing slightly to account for the longer horizons.
batch_queries: According to Wikipedia, will Hikaru Nakamura have an Elo rating on each resolution date listed below that's at least 1% higher than on 2025-11-09?
